reason: The remediation involves a simple configuration change to /etc/default/useradd to set the INACTIVE parameter, a standard password policy adjustment that follows STIG hardening patterns and does not affect critical services or system boot. It is unlikely to cause lockout or breakage if applied correctly.
estimated_impact: authentication/authorization affected; Potential accidental lockout of user accounts if INACTIVE value is set incorrectly; Requires careful testing on non-critical accounts
